target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): add reaction approval helpers
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Adds a shared `openclaw/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime` subpath for channel reaction approvals.
- Hardcodes the standard reaction decisions: 👍 allow-once, ♾️ allow-always, 👎 deny.
- Migrates WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage approval reaction rendering, binding, normalization, and target storage onto the SDK helper so channel code stops hand-rolling common approval text and emoji behavior.
- Keeps channel-specific routing/auth details in each channel while centralizing common pending prompt and local exec suppression behavior.
- Fixes Signal target-mode approval delivery so outbound plugin approval prompts register reaction targets before reactions are received.
- Removes the legacy WhatsApp send-level `/approve` parser/appender so reaction prompts must come from the native/shared approval renderer path.

Why does this matter now?

- WhatsApp and Signal already needed matching reaction approval semantics, and the old code duplicated prompt construction and reaction interpretation in each channel.
- Live Signal plugin target-mode testing showed that manual fallback prompts could be delivered without reaction target registration; this PR now binds those outbound approval prompts as reaction-capable targets.

What is the intended outcome?

- Existing channels can support exec/plugin reaction approvals through a common SDK abstraction, with manual `/approve` instructions still included at the end of the prompt.
- WhatsApp and Signal prod approval routes support 👍 allow-once and 👎 deny for exec/plugin approvals when enabled, and do not deliver approvals when disabled.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No channel config shape changes.
- Changelog includes a `(NOT_IN_SPEC)` hard-cut removal entry for deleting the legacy WhatsApp send-level reaction hint upgrader.
- No screenshots, videos, phone numbers, credentials, or live config committed to the repo.

What does success look like?

- The three migrated channels render standard reaction prompts, resolve reaction decisions consistently, and continue to pass their focused approval runtime tests.
- Live WhatsApp and Signal approval routes pass enabled allow/deny and disabled no-delivery scenarios for exec and plugin approvals.

What should reviewers focus on?

- SDK API shape and whether the remaining channel-specific code is only transport/auth/routing-specific.
- Signal's distinction between reaction-capable native delivery and manual fallback delivery.
- Signal target-mode prompt binding in `extensions/signal/src/send.ts` and `extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.ts`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Maintainer-requested local dev-loop work from the channel approval specs.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: shared SDK support for reaction-based exec/plugin approvals across WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage; Signal target-mode plugin approval prompts now register reaction targets.
- Real environment tested: Crabbox Linux remote validation, local focused approval runtime tests in this worktree, and live prod WhatsApp + Signal approval integration against the prod profile.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-native.test.ts`
  - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
  - `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs`
  - `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm build"` (`run_4ba89d1e4838`)
  - `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` (`run_70c74102e9e0`)
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/signal/src/send.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-native.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts`
  - `pnpm build`
  - `git diff --check`
  - `node .mem/main/proofs/demo-32-prod-whatsapp-signal-reaction-approvals/scripts/verify-results.mjs`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): focused Vitest reported 10 files / 121 tests passed before PR open; latest focused Signal rerun reported 3 files / 21 tests passed; latest focused WhatsApp/shared reaction rerun reported 4 files / 39 tests passed; SDK API baseline check passed; export check passed; Crabbox build `run_4ba89d1e4838` exited 0; Crabbox changed check `run_70c74102e9e0` exited 0; prod WhatsApp/Signal proof verifier reported `verified 12 approval scenarios`.
- Observed result after fix: migrated channels build and typecheck through changed gates, focused approval tests pass with the shared hardcoded reaction bindings, and live prod WhatsApp/Signal exec/plugin approval routes pass enabled allow, enabled deny, and disabled no-decision scenarios.
- What was not tested: live iMessage reaction round trip.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: live WhatsApp/Signal proof artifacts are local/redacted under `.mem/main/proofs/demo-32-prod-whatsapp-signal-reaction-approvals` and were not committed because PR artifacts/screenshots/live identifiers must not be pushed to the product repo.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): duplicated per-channel reaction prompt/binding code existed in WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage; Signal target-mode plugin approval prompts could be delivered without reaction target registration.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-native.test.ts`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm build"` (`run_4ba89d1e4838`)
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` (`run_70c74102e9e0`)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/signal/src/send.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-native.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check`
- `node .mem/main/proofs/demo-32-prod-whatsapp-signal-reaction-approvals/scripts/verify-results.mjs`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added SDK tests for hardcoded reaction bindings, emoji normalization, prompt payloads, target store behavior, and local exec suppression checks.
- Updated WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage reaction/native approval tests for shared SDK payloads and ♾️ allow-always behavior.
- Removed WhatsApp tests that asserted legacy send-level `/approve` prompt parsing/appending, matching the hard-cut removal.
- Added Signal coverage for target-mode outbound approval prompt reaction hinting, reaction target registration, and disabled route behavior.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- During precommit validation, `pnpm check:changed` caught stale test fixture types and one lint issue. Both were fixed before this PR was opened.
- During live Signal plugin integration, target-mode plugin approval prompts were delivered as manual `/approve` prompts without reaction target registration. The Signal send-layer binding fix addressed that, and the rerun passed.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added and updated.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Channels using native reaction approval prompts now share the same reaction prompt wording and include ♾️ allow-always when allowed.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Approval execution behavior is security-sensitive, but this change preserves channel auth/routing checks and centralizes reaction decision handling.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Incorrectly enabling a reaction binding where a channel cannot authenticate the reactor.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The SDK only handles generic reaction decisions, prompt content, and target storage; each channel still owns reactor authorization, route matching, and delivery eligibility.
- Signal target-mode reaction binding checks explicit approvers and top-level target route enablement before appending reaction hints or registering reaction targets.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Await CI and review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- None yet.
